ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-25-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg, via ZOOM, for Mary Hill-Melero, Administrator.

The court points to what appear to be duplicate requests for extraordinary fees in the time records submitted to the court. The court queries counsel regarding whether she would like to request a continuance to provide the court with documentation to support the petition. Counsel reports that she would like to discuss the preceding with her client, off the record. The court passes the matter. The matter is recalled. Counsel provides oral testimony and points the court to exhibits A and B which were submitted with the accounting petition. Regardless, counsel advises that her client would accept the court's ruling if it were confirmed that there are duplicate requests for fees. The court reviews the exhibits in open court and advises that it appears that the issue regarding the duplicate request for extraordinary fees has been clarified.

After additional discussion, the Accounting Petition is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Gomez. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (62 Carr Street, San Francisco, Ca 94124)
Off Calendar Mar-17-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg appeared by zoom for Mary Hill-Melero.
Eddie O'Sullivan (Agent for the Administrator) appeared by zoom.
Haley Kerstein (Agent for Cynthia Chen/current bid holder) appeared by zoom.

Ms. Friedeberg stated that Beneficiary Mackentral Williams was on zoom audio, but was unable to mute himself and could not check in with the Clerk, that he wanted her to inform the Court that he objects to the sale, that he has nowhere to live and that he wants the Court to appoint an Attorney for him. The Court did not grant the request and proceeded with the sale. The sale was confirmed at $623,000.00 to Trent Zhu of Better Homes and Gardens Real Estate on behalf of his Client Bernice Zhu based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed February 3, 2021. The Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property is under submission. The amount of the additional bond is to be calculated by Ms. Friedeberg. Ms. Friedeberg informed the Court that the overbidders were instructed to provide the deposit to the escrow company today, and that she will communicate with Mr. Zhu after the hearing.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-06-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg appeared by zoom for and with Mary Hill-Melero (Petitioner) by zoom.

The Court noted that no one is objecting to the petition. The Court also informed Ms. Friedeberg that the issue of equitable adoption needs to be a subject of a separate petition prior to final distribution. Ms. Friedeberg stated that the amount of the bond is based on the advisement of the Realtor as to what the property should be listed at. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order). Bond is set at $627,000.00. Status on close of estate is set for February 7, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
